status: complete — 30/30 generated, 29/30 valid (96.7%), gate (>=70%) PASSED; see experiment-results/REPORT.md
generation_note: 30 calls across two transient API keys (neither persisted in any committed file — verified by grep before every commit). The first key's validity window elapsed after 26/30 calls, surfacing as a final 401 authentication_error (a structured 4xx outcome, correctly not retried per transport_retry_policy — not a transport failure). The remaining 4 slots (tarefa-09-gen-03, tarefa-10-gen-01/02/03) were generated with a second key. Both keys were used transiently and the operator was advised to revoke them after use.

provenance.spec_baseline_note: Public commit of the frozen SPEC/AEP-IR-1.0.md; the report's baseline of record.
provenance.instrument_head_note: Published HEAD of the calibrated instrument (validator/runner/prompt/canonical). Read-only until the REPORT: any change re-gates (new GATE 0, new hash, new pilot). Advanced 0d347bc -> cc7dd41: a pre-generation re-gate that added the '## Edge Structure' schema clarification to prompt.md (source/target keys; from/to rejected) and regenerated all 30 pipeline/*/input-coder.md. Legitimate because NO real API generations existed yet — caught by the mechanics dry-run. This manifest commit is metadata-only, so no instrument file changed after cc7dd41; instrument_head remains cc7dd41.

canonicalization.note: Hard dependency (requirements.txt). The frozen Canonicalization Standard names RFC 8785; canonicaljson is NOT RFC 8785 and diverges on number serialization.

spec_fixable_rationale: Pre-registered BEFORE generation and frozen at GATE 0. Kept deliberately after reading the Origin Rules section of the frozen spec: OR-1 is a clear rule with three defined origins (producer, default, external scope), transmitted verbatim in prompt.md Rule 5, so a plan that still fails OR-1 is arguably violating an explicit instruction (leaning prompt/model). However, the structural trap remains a spec-design property — the class 'readonly + execution-scoped + no default' is unsatisfiable, so a generator reasoning semantically can fall into OR-1 in good faith. A conservative pre-registration that over-includes is defensible; editing the set after seeing results would be tampering. Decision: keep, deliberately.

generator.mechanism: scripted API, fresh isolated context per plan
generator.sampling: n/a — temperature/top_p/top_k removed on claude-opus-4-8; default sampling
generator.context: input-coder.md as sole user message; no system prompt additions; account memory disabled/absent (raw API call)
generator.transport_retry_policy: Pre-registered before Phase 2: a generated plan is data and is never retried. A transport failure with no content (network error, timeout, 5xx/529) is retried up to 3x, each attempt logged with its reason. Any HTTP error with a structured API error body (4xx) is final.
